definitions.reality: Not a static object, but a process that continuously organizes, runs, feeds back, reconfigures and carries itself forward.
definitions.runtime: The organizing process that continues within time, resources, constraints and feedback after Reality enters a carrier.
definitions.reality_grammar: The sixteen-stage generative logic through which Reality repeatedly moves from difference to continuity.
definitions.difference: The first distinguishable state through which Reality can appear.
definitions.constraint: A mechanism that narrows possibility so difference can acquire a sustainable direction.
definitions.structure: A sustainable organization of relationships formed as difference operates under constraint.
definitions.field: A sphere of influence formed by continuing connections among structures, shaping what is possible within it.
definitions.coordinate: A readable position that Reality acquires within networks of structure, relationship and direction.
definitions.navigation: The continuing process of locating and correcting direction based on Reality position, state, limits, direction and evidence.
definitions.projection: The form through which complex Reality enters experience after interface filtering, compression and organization; it is not equivalent to illusion or error.
definitions.projection_surface: The interface layer through which Reality can appear and be read.
definitions.projection_compression: The process through which Reality is filtered and simplified before entering limited perception and expression.
definitions.meaning: A structure of importance formed when a projection is interpreted, valued and connected to existing models.
definitions.shared_reality: A version of Reality maintained by multiple systems through projection, coordination and reinforcement.
definitions.synthetic_projection: A projection formed when artificial systems participate in filtering, generation, ranking or distribution.
definitions.temporal_medium: The operating condition that allows states to be ordered, activated, sustained and ended.
definitions.activation: The point at which time and conditions open the entrance for Reality to begin running.
definitions.runtime_capability: A capability called by Reality for orientation, understanding, expression, resources, execution, relationship, survival or movement.
definitions.runtime_driver: A driving force that makes Reality respond preferentially to a particular direction, pressure or need.
definitions.motion: Change occurring through archetypes such as carrying, initiation, permeation, flow, differentiation, boundary formation and exchange.
definitions.runtime_state: The current organization of Reality within a specific time, condition, carrier and Runtime network.
definitions.feedback: The process through which operating results return to the system and influence the next formation of Reality.
definitions.settlement: The process through which feedback gradually becomes a stable foundation for the next Reality cycle.
definitions.reconfiguration: The reorganization of an existing structure under new conditions.
definitions.emergence: New organization appearing after reconfiguration that cannot be independently explained by any single local part.
definitions.continuity: The capacity of a Reality pattern to keep running across time, state or carrier.
definitions.carrier: The supporting structure that allows Reality to enter a specific living being or system and continue running.
definitions.carrier_compression: The process through which Reality is converted into a configuration that a limited carrier can support.
definitions.reality_initialization_state: The foundational Runtime state formed by initial conditions, time, body and configuration when life begins to run; it is not a fixed destiny.
definitions.runtime_medium: Conditions such as light, air, water, food, sound, temperature and space through which Reality enters and regulates living operation.
definitions.carrier_stability: The capacity of a carrier to preserve enough integrity and coordination through change to continue running.
definitions.runtime_maintenance: The process through which a carrier sustains continuous operation by recovery, regulation and coordination.
definitions.runtime_drift: The gradual movement of long-running operation outside its prior stable range under pressure, noise, cost or mismatched feedback.
definitions.experience: The state in which Reality enters consciousness and becomes content that a subject can feel.
definitions.awareness: The range in which experience becomes recognizable and observable by consciousness.
definitions.self_model: The compressed model consciousness forms of its own continuity, position, boundaries and identity.
definitions.emotion: An experiential signal formed by consciousness from state, prediction, value and preparation for action.
definitions.expression: The stage at which experience begins to appear outwardly through language, behavior, media or silence.
definitions.agency: The stage in which expression enters choice, decision, responsibility and commitment, and begins to affect Reality.
definitions.identity: A stable organization formed through settled action and feedback that continues to influence understanding and choice.
definitions.conscious_continuity: The capacity for experience, self-model, meaning, expression and identity to remain integrable across time.
